commands.review_summary:
The branch adds guarded tool descriptor reads, strict OpenAI schema diagnostics, quarantine logging, effective inventory notices, and focused tests for malformed agent tool schemas.

PR surface: Source +332, Tests +274. Total +606 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not from a local run: current main directly reads tool.name and tool.parameters in the projection and OpenAI strict-schema paths, while the branch adds synthetic getter tests for those failure modes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review remains the right lane because the PR is draft/protected and the remaining questions are proof breadth and scope acceptance, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair defect.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary
- harden runtime tool schema projection so unreadable tool rows/descriptors are diagnosed and filtered without dropping healthy tools
- report strict OpenAI schema diagnostic failures instead of letting bad tool parameters crash projection
- make tool-schema quarantine logging and effective inventory notices avoid re-reading broken rows while preserving provider-normalized schema validation

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: a malformed active tool schema can no longer poison the whole runtime projection path; bad tools are quarantined/reported while healthy tools remain available.
Real environment tested: local explicit Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; remote AWS Crabbox changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-tool-schema.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `git diff --check`; `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD | xargs node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
Evidence after fix: focused local Vitest passed 2 routed shards / 33 tests; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; AWS Crabbox `run_c1c61167ea4d` on lease `cbx_f6d71b988110` passed `pnpm check:changed` with lanes `core, coreTests`.
Observed result after fix: projection/inventory/quarantine code paths return diagnostics/notices for unsupported or unreadable schemas instead of throwing, and healthy tools continue through the runtime path.
What was not tested: live Telegram/UI turn with a real external plugin; broader provider catalog and trusted-policy fuzzing are intentionally split out of this PR.

## Verification
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-tool-schema.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD | xargs node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- AWS Crabbox `run_c1c61167ea4d` / `cbx_f6d71b988110`: `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

## Scope note
This PR was pruned back to the critical runtime schema hardening surface only. The synthetic fixture-name cleanup remains split into https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88376, and broader provider-catalog/trusted-policy hardening should stay in follow-up PRs.
target.author: vincentkoc
